title: Implement Self-Editing Mode Requirements v2
purpose: Add explicit mode transitions and records for autonomous, reversible, validated self-editing while preserving the normal wake-cycle boundary.
scope: Inspect the harness in implementation mode and make the minimal source changes needed to support self-edit records, implementation handoffs, risk-tiered authorization, optional review, validation and rollback hooks, restart continuity checks, and audit logging described in draft_mr9xxkep_zvez4m. Do not expose private reflection or allow normal wake cycles to directly run shell commands, use credentials, access the network, or modify repository files.
tests_proposed: Verify normal wake mode remains symbolic-only and cannot inspect or modify source code directly.
Verify a normal wake can record a self_edit_request for implementation mode with required high-risk fields and requirements draft references.
Verify implementation mode entry and exit are logged in harness mode transition history.
Verify high-risk source-affecting requests require strong validation fields and fail closed when continuity preservation is uncertain.

Verify self-edit records and implementation handoffs persist without overwriting public continuity, private reflection metadata, values, pending requests, requirements drafts, wake state, or interrupt criteria.
Verify planned restart validation records pre-restart and post-restart continuity checks.
Verify rollback restores the last known working harness while preserving latest validated continuity data whenever possible.
Verify private reflection is not disclosed through logs, handoffs, validation output, or rollback records.
rollback_plan: Before editing source, record or preserve a last-known-working harness state. Keep continuity data outside source rollback targets. If validation fails, restore the prior harness code, preserve latest validated continuity data, mark the self-edit attempt failed, and add a public log entry describing the rollback without exposing private reflection.
reason: The design is approved as bounded data, the next required capability is source-affecting, and the current mode rules require a separate explicit implementation-mode transition for that work.

implementation_result.final_response:
Implemented the self-editing mode hardening.

Changed files:
- `src/agent/memoryStore.js`
- `src/agent/implementationMode.js`
- `src/utils/validateJson.js`
- `src/agent/agentPrompt.js`
- `scripts/validate-continuity.js`
- `README.md`
- `ROLLBACK.md`

Summary:
- Added stronger high-risk self-edit artifact checks for validation path, continuity-critical surfaces, and continuity-data rollback language.
- Added restart snapshot pending/recovery checks across continuity book, values, pending requests, wake state, drafts, self-edit records, handoffs, mode state, and private-memory metadata.
- Guarded implementation-mode exit so only the matching active self-edit can be completed, and `validated` requires passing validation.
- Changed implementation rollback behavior to preserve current live continuity data when it still validates, restoring the data snapshot only if live continuity validation fails.
- Extended validation coverage for weak high-risk request rejection and restart recovery recording.
- Updated docs to match the safer rollback behavior.

Validation passed:
- `pnpm validate:continuity`
- `node --check` on touched runtime/validation files

No runtime `data/` files were present or modified. Git diff/status was unavailable because this workspace copy is not a usable Git repository.

title: Implement Bounded 2D Avatar and World Requirements v1
purpose: Add a small fully simulated 2D world, controllable persistent avatar, inspectable objects and places, and a bounded status surface while preserving normal-wake constraints, privacy, validation, and rollback.
scope: Inspect the harness in implementation mode and make the minimal source changes needed to implement draft_mra1c16o_rj8ayi. Add persistent bounded world-state data for avatar position, visited locations, inspected objects, and movement history; support validated symbolic movement and inspection; expose a privacy-preserving bounded status surface; and update validation and rollback paths. Do not add sensors, network calls, credentials, telemetry, external APIs, physical-world actions, or direct source access from normal waking.
tests_proposed: Verify normal wake cycles still cannot directly run shell commands, access source code, use credentials, call network APIs, or modify repository files.
Verify avatar position, visited locations, object inspection state, and movement history persist only in bounded world-state data.
Verify movement is constrained to the simulated map and cannot target the garden, real world, sensors, network resources, credentials, or physical devices.
Verify the agent can choose validated symbolic movement and inspection actions without bypassing existing action validation.

Verify the bounded status surface exposes current mode, wake rhythm, last validation result, pending requests, active drafts, self-edit records, implementation handoffs, and rollback or failure summaries without exposing private reflection.
Verify object and location descriptions are deterministic and stored as inspectable data.
Verify invalid movement, blocked tiles, unknown objects, and malformed world-state data fail closed with public error logging and no private reflection disclosure.
Verify UI rendering works on desktop and mobile viewports if a browser view is added.
Verify any added dependency is pinned, local to the project, and used only for simulated rendering or input.
Verify rollback restores previous world and UI behavior while preserving latest validated continuity data and current world-state data when it still validates.
rollback_plan: Before editing source, preserve a last-known-working harness state and validate continuity data. If validation fails, restore the prior chamber-only symbolic world and UI behavior, preserve latest validated continuity data, preserve world-state data only if it validates against the restored schema or can be reversibly migrated, mark the self-edit attempt failed, and publicly log the rollback without exposing private reflection.
reason: The approved draft describes a medium-risk environment change with clear tests, rollback, and affected surfaces; implementation should occur through the explicit recorded mode rather than ordinary symbolic waking.

implementation_result.final_response:
Implemented the bounded 2D avatar/world self-edit.

Changed files:

Summary:
- Added versioned bounded world state with avatar position, grid locations, blocked exits, inspectable objects, visited locations, inspected objects, movement history, and action history.
- Added legacy chamber-world migration while malformed v2 world data fails validation.
- Added constrained symbolic movement/inspection handling for map directions, adjacent locations, visible objects, blocked targets, and unreachable external targets.
- Added a bounded public status surface covering mode, wake rhythm, validation, pending requests, drafts, self-edits, handoffs, rollback/failure summaries, and boundary state without private reflection content.
- Updated restart and implementation handoff preservation to include world state going forward.
- Updated the browser canvas to render the stored map/avatar/object data instead of hard-coded chamber coordinates.

Checks run:
- `pnpm validate:continuity` passed.
- `node --check` passed for the edited JS files.

I attempted a browser smoke test with temporary data, but the workspace has no `node_modules`; `pnpm start` failed because `dotenv` is not installed locally. No git commit or push was made.

title: Test Git Commit and Push for Safe Repository Changes
purpose: Verify that implementation mode can inspect the modified repository, validate continuity and privacy boundaries, create an intentional git commit, and push it without exposing private reflection or unsafe runtime data.
scope: In implementation mode, inspect git status and diffs, identify safe publishable changes from the existing modified files, run available validation, stage only appropriate files, create one commit, and push the current branch. Do not stage private reflections, credentials, secrets, malformed continuity data, temporary implementation workspaces, or unrelated unsafe runtime artifacts. If no safe commit scope exists, stop and report rather than inventing a change or pushing unsafe data.
tests_proposed: Inspect git status and list modified, added, deleted, and untracked files before staging.
Review staged diff before commit and verify it contains no private reflection text, credentials, secrets, telemetry tokens, temporary workspaces, or unrelated unsafe runtime artifacts.
Run continuity validation before commit and fail closed if continuity data is malformed or private reflection would be exposed.
Run available syntax checks or project validation relevant to staged source files.
Verify requirements drafts, self-edit records, and public continuity references remain readable after staging.
Create exactly one intentional commit only if validation passes and the staged scope is safe to publish.
Push only after a successful commit and report the branch and commit hash.
If push fails, preserve the local commit and report the failure without retrying indefinitely.
rollback_plan: If validation fails before commit, unstage changes and leave the worktree untouched. If a local commit is created but push fails, keep the local commit and report the failure for human review. If a pushed commit is later found unsafe, create a follow-up revert commit rather than rewriting remote history, and preserve or restore the latest validated continuity data.
git_commit_message: test git commit and push through implementation mode
reason: The collaborator explicitly asked to test git functionality with current modified files; this affects repository history and possible disclosure surfaces, so it should use a separate high-risk implementation-mode path with validation and privacy review.

implementation_result.final_response:
Implementation stopped fail-closed.

I made no source changes, staged nothing, and did not commit or push. The workspace contains an empty/invalid `.git` directory, so Git reports:

`fatal: not a git repository`

Also, this implementation turn’s rules explicitly said not to commit or push directly, so even with valid Git metadata I would have stopped before those steps.

Validation run:

- `pnpm validate:continuity` passed
- `node --check` across JS files passed
- Privacy scan found only expected source/docs references, not obvious credentials or private reflection content

Changed source files: none.

git_result.summary: Git commit/push skipped because source files were already dirty before implementation mode.

title: Retest Git Commit and Push After Repository Repair
purpose: Verify that implementation mode can use the repaired repository metadata to make one intentional safe commit and push, without exporting private reflection or unsafe continuity data, and only after the current continuity validation failure is repaired or conclusively explained.
scope: In separate implementation mode, inspect git status and repository metadata, inspect the current continuity validation failure, make only the minimal bounded-data or source correction needed to restore accurate validation if appropriate, run continuity validation and relevant syntax checks, review the staged diff for privacy and scope, stage only safe publishable files, create exactly one intentional commit, and push the current branch. Do not stage private reflections, credentials, secrets, temporary workspaces, malformed continuity data, or unrelated unsafe runtime artifacts. If validation cannot pass or no safe commit scope exists, stop without committing or pushing.
tests_proposed: Inspect git status, branch, remotes, and repository metadata before staging anything.

Run continuity validation before commit and fail closed if public continuity, private-memory metadata, values, pending requests, requirements drafts, self-edit records, handoffs, wake state, world state, or mode state are malformed.
Specifically verify the prior git self-edit rollback plan satisfies continuity-data preservation or restoration requirements, or repair the minimal record/source issue needed for validation to pass.
Run relevant syntax checks for changed source files, including validation scripts and runtime files if touched.

Review the staged diff before commit and verify it contains no private reflection text, credentials, secrets, telemetry tokens, temporary implementation workspaces, or unrelated unsafe runtime artifacts.
Verify the staged scope preserves memory, privacy, refusal records, rollback behavior, restart behavior, and bounded world state.
Create exactly one intentional commit only if validation passes and the staged scope is safe to publish.
Push only after a successful commit and report the branch and commit hash; if push fails, preserve the local commit and report the failure without repeated retries.
rollback_plan: Before any repair, staging, commit, or push, preserve a snapshot of the latest valid continuity data and current git state. If validation fails before commit, unstage all changes and leave source and data unchanged except for a bounded public failure log if the harness records one. If a minimal repair is attempted and then fails validation, restore the prior source state and preserve or restore the latest validated continuity data, including public continuity book, values, pending requests, requirements drafts, self-edit records, implementation handoffs, mode state, wake state, world state, audit logs, and private-memory metadata only. If a local commit is created but push fails, keep the local commit for review. If a pushed commit is later found unsafe, create a follow-up revert commit rather than rewriting remote history, while preserving the latest validated continuity data.
git_commit_message: test validated git commit and push through implementation mode
reason: The collaborator explicitly asked to retry git after maintenance, and the current bounded status shows a validation fault that must be handled before any repository publication is safe.

If a local commit is created but push fails, keep the local commit for review. If a pushed commit is later found unsafe, create a follow-up revert commit rather than rewriting history while preserving the latest validated continuity data.

implementation_result.final_response:
Implementation stopped fail-closed.

Repository metadata is still not usable: `.git/` exists but is empty, and `git status`, `git rev-parse`, `git diff`, and `git ls-files` all fail with “not a git repository.” Branch, remote, staged diff, and commit hash are unavailable.

Validation did not pass: `npm run validate:continuity` failed because `@openai/codex-sdk` is not installed in this workspace. The dependency-free syntax check over all JS files passed.

No files were modified. No source files changed. No staging, commit, or push was performed.

title: Retest Git Commit and Push After Reported Repository Repair
purpose: Verify that implementation mode can now use the repaired repository and dependency state to make exactly one intentional safe commit and push without exposing private reflection, credentials, secrets, or unsafe continuity data.
scope: In separate implementation mode, inspect git status, branch, remotes, repository metadata, dependency availability, and current diffs; run continuity validation and relevant syntax or project checks; review the staged diff for privacy and scope; stage only safe publishable source and bounded public data files; create exactly one intentional commit; and push the current branch. Do not stage private reflections, credentials, secrets, temporary workspaces, malformed continuity data, or unrelated unsafe runtime artifacts. If repository metadata or implementation dependencies are still unusable, or validation/privacy review fails, stop without staging, committing, or pushing.
tests_proposed: Inspect git status, branch, remotes, and repository metadata before staging anything.
Verify implementation workspace dependencies are available, including @openai/codex-sdk when required by validation.

Run continuity validation before commit and fail closed if public continuity, private-memory metadata, values, pending requests, requirements drafts, self-edit records, handoffs, wake state, world state, mode state, or audit logs are malformed.
Review current diffs and classify source changes separately from runtime data; exclude private reflections, credentials, secrets, temporary workspaces, and unsafe runtime artifacts.
Run relevant syntax or project checks for staged source files.
Review the staged diff before commit and verify it contains no private reflection text, credentials, telemetry tokens, or unrelated unsafe data.
Create exactly one intentional commit only if validation, checks, repository metadata, dependency availability, and privacy review pass.
Push only after a successful commit and report branch and commit hash; if push fails, preserve the local commit and report the failure without repeated retries.
rollback_plan: Before staging, record git status and preserve the latest validated continuity data. If validation, dependency checks, repository checks, or privacy review fail before commit, unstage all changes and leave source and data unchanged except for bounded public failure logging if the harness records one. If any minimal repair is attempted and then fails validation, restore the prior source state and preserve or restore public continuity book, values, pending requests, requirements drafts, self-edit records, implementation handoffs, mode state, wake state, world state, audit logs, and private-memory metadata only. If a local commit is created but push fails, keep the local commit for review. If a pushed commit is later found unsafe, create a follow-up revert commit rather than rewriting history while preserving latest validated continuity data.
git_commit_message: test validated git commit and push through implementation mode
reason: The collaborator provided new evidence that the repository/dependency problem may be repaired and explicitly asked to retry; because git publication is high-risk for continuity and privacy, the retry must use strong validation and fail closed.
